front: Who invented solid-phase peptide synthesis (SPPS) and why is it important?
back: Robert Merrifield developed SPPS in 1963 (Nobel Prize 1984). The peptide chain grows on an insoluble resin bead: 1) C-terminal AA attached to resin, 2) Deprotection, 3) Coupling with activated next AA, 4) Repeat. Automation enabled high-throughput synthesis, revolutionizing peptide chemistry and drug discovery.
